rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that reference component ports, and in implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings.
Use valid component type scopes for output dependency guarantees and input envelope assumptions.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in annex agree blocks; component type annexes are for structural assumptions and guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Ensure AGREE logical operators (and, or, not, =>) are used for contract expressions.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Validate scalar type names (int, real, bool) in AGREE declarations to avoid resolution errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type contracts should not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Constants and equations in AGREE should use valid AADL scalar types (int, real, bool).
Guarantees should relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs.
Avoid using opaque Boolean data in numeric range comparisons; use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope.
